summary: Follow-up PR that corrected the build/link configuration for the Lucky item source by marking melee/it/items/itlucky.c as Matching instead of NonMatching in configure.py. The body says this was an "Oops" follow-up to PR #2088, so the practical impact was ensuring the newly matched itlucky object participates in the matching build/link path.
what_changed: In configure.py under MatchingFor(*versions), the Object entry for melee/it/items/itlucky.c was changed from Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itlucky.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itlucky.c").
No source code changes were included in this slice; the PR only updates the matching/link classification for itlucky.c.
The PR body identifies this as a follow-up to PR #2088, implying the matching work landed there but the configure.py status was not updated at that time.
smart_moves: Kept the follow-up minimal: a one-line configure.py change with no unrelated churn.
Used the existing item object list convention, placing itlucky.c among neighboring Pokémon/item files and only flipping the Matching/NonMatching enum.
Quickly corrected a missed link/matching-status update after the prior PR, reducing the chance that a matched file remains excluded from the matching build configuration.
decomp_lessons: When a file becomes matching, remember to update configure.py from NonMatching to Matching; source-level matching is not complete until the build configuration links it as matching.
Small post-merge follow-ups can be appropriate for missed matching status flips, but they should remain tightly scoped and explicit.
For item decomp work, verify both the source file match and its Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/...c") entry in the relevant MatchingFor configuration list.
naming_conventions: Item source paths use lowercase item identifiers under melee/it/items/, e.g. itlucky.c, itmew.c, itcerebi.c, itporygon2.c, itmaril.c.
Build object status is expressed through Object(Matching, "path") versus Object(NonMatching, "path") in configure.py.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactic is visible in this PR slice; the only evidence is the configuration flip from NonMatching to Matching.
The practical matching tactic demonstrated is to audit configure.py after matching a file so the matched object is actually linked in the matching configuration.
follow_up_queries: Check PR #2088 for the actual itlucky.c decomp/matching changes that this PR links.
Search for other item files left as Object(NonMatching) after source-level matching PRs.
Audit configure.py item object entries for consistency between matched source files and Matching status.
